pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 10 defines SOURCE_FILE="$SOURCE_DIR/marketing_hero_source.png" where SOURCE_DIR="/home/ga/Pictures/RawAssets"; export_result.sh line 10 confirms this path
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 9 defines OUTPUT_DIR="/home/ga/Documents/WebReady"; export_result.sh line 8 confirms same path

Code analysis: task.json description states 'Resize the image to a width of exactly 1280 pixels'; verifier.py line 44 loads 'target_width' from metadata with default 1280; export_result.sh line 40 checks the width dimension

Code analysis: task.json description states 'set the compression format to WebP'; verifier.py line 68 checks for 'WEBP' format string from ImageMagick's identify command; Web search confirms Squoosh.app supports WebP export format
Code analysis: verifier.py line 45 loads 'width_tolerance' from metadata with default 2; line 76-78 checks if abs(actual_width - target_width) <= width_tolerance
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 29 echo states 'Generating high-res source asset (3840x2160)...'; line 31 uses ImageMagick convert command with '-size 3840x2160' flag
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 10 shows filename ends in .png extension; line 36 shows ImageMagick saves to .png file

Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 28-40 show image is generated using 'convert' command with gradient, noise, text, and shapes rather than downloaded from external URL. Fallback uses dummyimage.com but primary method is local generation
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 53 checks history database for '%squoosh.app%'; Web search confirms https://squoosh.app is the official Google-created image optimization tool hosted at this URL

privileged_info_summary: The task requires optimizing a synthetic 3840x2160 PNG image generated by ImageMagick using Google's Squoosh.app. The target specifications are: resize to exactly 1280 pixels width (±2 pixel tolerance), convert to WebP format, and save output to /home/ga/Documents/WebReady/hero_optimized.webp. The source image must be located at /home/ga/Pictures/RawAssets/marketing_hero_source.png. All metadata values in task.json are accurate and match the implementation in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py. The verifier checks: file existence and creation timestamp, WebP format, correct width dimensions, compression ratio (expecting >50% reduction), and browser history confirmation of squoosh.app visit.
